content.Text: [Wed 2026-04-22 06:35 PDT] Create a new interactive Maximum Independent Set Finder page at `docs/independent-set.html` for this GraphVisual project. ## What is Maximum Independent Set? An independent set is a set of vertices where no two are adjacent. The Maximum Independent Set (MIS) is the largest such set. It's NP-hard, complementary to Maximum Clique (independent set of G = clique of complement of G). ## Style & Structure Follow the EXACT same patterns as `docs/dominating-set.html` and `docs/vertex-cover.html` — same CSS classes, same layout structure, same sidebar navigation, same control panel style. Use `link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css"` and inline styles matching those pages. The sidebar must include ALL existing links from the other pages (check dominating-set.html for the full list) plus add a new entry for this page: ```html 🏝️Independent Set ``` Place it after the "Self-Repair" link. ## Features to implement: ### 1. Three Algorithms - **Greedy** — iteratively pick the vertex with minimum degree among remaining candidates, add to set, remove it and its neighbors - **Exact (Backtracking)** — branch and bound to find true maximum independent set. Use pruning: if current + remaining candidates ≤ best found, prune. Limit to ~18 vertices for exact. - **Complement-Clique** — build complement graph, run Bron-Kerbosch to find max clique, which equals the MIS of the original ### 2. Step-by-step Animation - Each algorithm step is recorded and can be played/paused/stepped through - Speed slider (50ms to 1500ms) - Step counter and history list showing each decision ### 3. Canvas Visualization - Draw graph on HTML5 Canvas with the same style as dominating-set.html - Nodes in independent set: bright green (#00e676) filled - Candidate nodes being considered: yellow (#ffd740) - Eliminated nodes (adjacent to set member): gray with reduced opacity - Normal nodes: default color - Edges between independent set nodes and their eliminated neighbors: highlighted - Drag nodes to reposition - Hover tooltips showing node degree and status ### 4. 8 Presets 1. **Triangle (K3)** — MIS = 1 2. **Path P5** — 5-node path, MIS = 3 3. **Cycle C6** — 6-node cycle, MIS = 3 4. **Petersen** — Petersen graph (10 nodes), MIS = 4 5. **Bipartite K3,3** — complete bipartite, MIS = 3 6. **Star K1,5** — star graph, MIS = 5 (all leaves) 7. **Crown Graph** — 8 vertices (crown/cocktail party complement), MIS = 4 8. **Random (12 nodes)** — random graph ### 5. Text Input Allow users to enter edges as adjacency list text, same format as dominating-set.html ### 6. Stats Bar - Independent Set Size (α) - Total Vertices - Total Edges - Density - Algorithm used - Steps taken ### 7. Relationship Panel A small info box showing: - α(G) + β(G) = n (Gallai's theorem: independence number + vertex cover number = n) - α(G) = ω(Ḡ) (independence number = clique number of complement) - Show computed values for the current graph ### 8. Export Button to copy the independent set as JSON ## Important Technical Notes - All in ONE self-contained HTML file (except styles.css link) - NO external JS dependencies - Canvas-based rendering with requestAnimationFrame - Dark theme matching the project's style - Mobile-responsive sidebar collapse - The page must work standalone when opened in browser ## Also update `docs/index.html` Add a card for the Independent Set Finder in the tools grid. Look at how other tools are listed and add a matching entry with icon 🏝️ and a brief description. ## Build verification After creating the files, verify the HTML is well-formed by checking it contains closing tag and the file is non-empty.
I’m matching the new page to the existing tool pages first, then I’ll mirror their structure into `docs/independent-set.html` and add the matching card in `docs/index.html`.

👑 Dominating Set Finder Find minimum dominating sets with greedy & exact algorithms

A dominating set S of a graph G is a subset of vertices such that every vertex is either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Finding a minimum dominating set is NP-hard, but greedy approximation works well in practice. This tool lets you visualize both approaches step by step.

About Dominating Sets

📡

Wireless Networks

Dominating sets model optimal placement of relay nodes — every device is within one hop of a relay.

🏥

Facility Location

Place facilities (hospitals, fire stations) so every community is adjacent to at least one.

🔒

Network Monitoring

Position sensors to observe all network links — a dominating set monitors every neighbor.

🧬

Biological Networks

Identify essential proteins in PPI networks: a dominating set covers all interaction partners.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

🛡️ Minimum Vertex Cover Finder Find minimum vertex covers with greedy, exact & König algorithms

A vertex cover of a graph G is a set S of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in S. Finding a minimum vertex cover is NP-hard, but a simple greedy 2-approximation exists. For bipartite graphs, König's theorem gives an exact solution in polynomial time via maximum matching.

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
🏗️

Architecture

Project structure, data pipeline, algorithm details, component design.
🗄️

Database Schema

PostgreSQL table schemas, query patterns, data pipeline ordering.
📖

API Reference

Detailed class and method documentation for all public APIs.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT
